title: styphelia pomarae
intro: Styphelia pomarae, also known as Mairene or Tahina, is a small shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Ericaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Styphelia pomarae has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it a useful remedy for various health issues, including wound healing and reducing pain. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, and it is also used in modern herbal medicine preparations, such as capsules and tinctures, to support overall health and well-being.
